title: GPTs and Custom Assistants
content:
- Altman and Gates Podcast
- When you look at the next two years, what do you think some of the key milestones will be?
- Multimodality will definitely be important. Which means speech in, speech out? Speech in, speech out. Images. Eventually video. Clearly, people really want that. We’ve launched images and audio, and it had a much stronger response than we expected.
- We’ll be able to push that much further, but maybe the most important areas of progress will be around reasoning ability. Right now, GPT-4 can reason in only extremely limited ways.
- Also reliability. If you ask GPT-4 most questions 10,000 times, one of those 10,000 is probably pretty good, but it doesn’t always know which one, and you’d like to get the best response of 10,000 each time, and so that increase in reliability will be important.
- Customizability and personalization will also be very important. People want very different things out of GPT-4: different styles, different sets of assumptions. We’ll make all that possible, and then also the ability to have it use your own data. The ability to know about you, your email, your calendar, how you like appointments booked, connected to other outside data sources, all of that. Those will be some of the most important areas of improvement. In the basic algorithm right now, it’s just feed forward, multiply, and so to generate every new word, it’s essentially doing the same thing.
- Custom Assistants
- [SamurAIGPT/Open-Custom-GPT: Create Custom GPT and add/embed on your site using Assistants api (github.com)](https://github.com/SamurAIGPT/Open-Custom-GPT)

- # Project [[Could]]
- Custom GPT
  - Tags database for logseq
  - receive an unstructured page
  - expand all links with summaries
  - group by
  - subgroup (identified by gpt)
  - date (old new)
  - create narrative scaffold around the links
  - restructure pass
- Custom API assistant
  - Convert using assistants
  - move summary code to locahost
  - integrate into logseq right clock
- Custom voice to 3d model through API calls
